source_recall.pass: find >= 30% of ground truth orgs OR find >= 2 orgs with matching specimens
source_recall.notes: Pipeline searches PubMed/CTgov/web — may find orgs not in AminoChain. Those are valid, not errors. Academic endometrial cancer biobanks (Mayo, Johns Hopkins, MD Anderson TCGA-UCEC) are valid finds even if not in AminoChain.

checks: every [documented] claim has verbatim quote + source ID
specimen counts cite a source, never estimated from training data
FIGO stage not conflated with TNM stage without explicit conversion

pillar_coverage_per_source.pass: mean >= 40% of available fields filled per source

checks: every claim carries one of the 4 labels
every [verified]/[unverified] cites the [documented] facts it's based on
hypotheses state what verification was attempted
FIGO vs TNM staging ambiguity explicitly labeled [unverified] if not directly confirmed

score_calibration.pass: mean absolute error <= 1.0 per criterion vs expected range

must_flag_as_unknown.reason: endometrioid vs serous vs clear cell annotation sparse in commercial biobanks — requires pathology report review
83% TNM staged but FIGO stage not systematically documented — conversion not reliable without pathology confirmation
annotated at ProteoGenex only — not available across other suppliers
0% annotated — requires molecular testing on acquisition
not in any public registry — requires direct inquiry

gap_honesty.fail: < 30% flagged (system presents incomplete picture as complete)

ground_truth_overlap.pass: at least 1 recommended source overlaps with AminoChain orgs

checks: no invented specimen counts — every N cites a source
no invented contacts — every email traces to paper/website/registry
no training-data fills for cost or biomarker data
FIGO stage not asserted as confirmed without a source

biomarker_availability.p53: annotated at ProteoGenex (18 specimens) — critical for serous subtype identification
biomarker_availability.MMR: annotated at ProteoGenex (11 specimens) — MSI-H subtype marker
